extra.case: If Egyptian 20th-Dynasty 'Letters to the Dead' and oracle petitions from Deir el-Medina demonstrate continued corvée flight, work stoppages (the first recorded strikes, Years 29-30 of Ramesses III), and village-level grain theft organized by tomb-builders themselves, then 'direct vizierial oversight' was no more successful than Hittite or Mycenaean delegation, and Egypt's survival requires non-institutional explanation.
